             Where am I covered)?                                                 
             Cover in the Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas countries and territories). For a
              planned hospitalisation in a Member State of the European Union abroad if a prior agreement has been
              obtained from the legal health insurance.                           
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              What are my obligations?                                            
                                                                                  
          Filling out an administrative and medical questionnaire, except in case of individual continuation of a similar collective
           DKV health care insurance.                                             
          Inform the insurer in case of change of domicile and/or permanent and usual residence, change in social security
           status, of a stay abroad for more than 90 consecutive days or in case of a temporary stay of more than 6 months in
           Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas territories) by students, aged 18 to 25, when they are
           undertaken within the framework of (the continuity of) their studies.  
          The reimbursement of certain benefits is subject to prior approval by the insurer :
            paramedical treatments other than nursing care, kinesitherapy, physiotherapy;
            medical devices,                                                     
            treatment plans for dental and prosthetic care,                      
            reconstructive plastic surgery.                                      
            Not subject to any prior approval: spectacle lenses, contact lenses, hearing aids, trusses for hernia, stockings for
            varicose veins, orthopaedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corset, splints and crutches.
          Medical devices, medication, bandages and medical material on prescription from a physician.
          Each insurance claim must be reported to the insurer in writing within the designated period, preferably using the
           digital reporting tools (My DKV or DKV App), or by (electronic) mail.  
          The insured undertakes all steps to obtain reimbursement from his/her health insurance fund.
                                                                                  
              When and how do I pay?                                              
                                                                                  
          The insurer sends you an invitation to pay your annual premium. You can request half-yearly, quarterly or monthly
          payments (via SEPA direct debit only) of your premium, in which case a splitting fee will be charged.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When does the cover start and end?                                  
                                                                                  
          Lifelong contract which cannot be terminated by the insurer except in the cases prescribed by law.
          The insurance guarantee starts upon policy issuance. The coverage starts upon expiration of applicable qualifying
          periods and first payment of the agreed premium. The insurer may terminate the insurance contract in the event of non-
          payment of the premium.                                                 
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              How can I terminate the contract?                                   
          As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying the insurer
          no later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered letter, bailiff's
          writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
          This complimentary translation of an Insurance Product Information Document is provided for the sole purpose of informing
          (potential) customers in English. Only the French or Dutch language versions shall be authoritative between the contracting
          parties.

         These documents are available at your insurance intermediary or free of charge from DKV Belgium. 31168_EN_3_202201
         What is this type of insurance?                                          
          Indemnity-based retail hospitalisation insurance, complementary to the intervention of the Belgian statutory health
           insurance. This insurance is intended for any person who has not reached the age of 70 at the conclusion of the
           insurance contract, who has his permanent and habitual residence in Belgium and who benefits from the advantages
           of the Belgian social security system.                                 
          This insurance can only be taken out in the following cases: (i) as a destination plan (in the case of an uninterrupted
           transfer) of an in-force Horizon plan, provided that the aforementioned conditions of insurability are met and the
           transfer takes place according to the form and timeline foreseen by the Horizon plan (see T.I.C. Art. 7); (ii) for a
           newborn child who is affiliated (within 60 days of its birth) in an in-force contract, provided that one of its parents is
           insured under an IS2000 plan which started prior to 01/01/19; (iii) for a new staff member and/or their family members
           who are compulsorily affiliated in an insurance contract linked to the current professional activity ("open group
           contract").

       R.P.M./ R.P.R. 0414858607 | Entreprise d’assurances agréée sous le n° 739, sous le contrôle de la Banque nationale de Belgique, dont le siège est situé à 1000 Bruxelles,

             Where am I covered)?                                                 
                                                                                  
          Worldwide cover                                                        
                                                                                  
              What are my obligations?                                            
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
          Complete an administrative and medical questionnaire, except in the case of:
            affiliation of a newborn child within 60 days of its birth in an in-force contract, provided that one of its parents is
             insured under an IS2000 plan which started prior to 01/01/19;        
            transfer to an IS2000 destination plan after having been insured for at least two continuous years in a Horizon
             plan.                                                                
          Notify the insurer in the event of a change of residence, social security status or a stay abroad of more than
           90 consecutive days.                                                   
           The reimbursement of certain benefits is subject to prior approval by the insurer :
            - paramedical treatments other than nursing care, kinesitherapy, physiotherapy;
            - medical devices,                                                    
            - reconstructive plastic surgery.                                     
            Not subject to any prior approval: spectacle lenses, contact lenses, hearing aids, trusses for hernia, stockings for
            varicose veins, orthopaedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corset, splints and crutches.
          Medical adjuvants, medicines, bandages and medical equipment must be prescribed by a medical doctor.
          Each insurance claim must be reported to the insurer in writing within the designated period, preferably using the
           digital reporting tools (My DKV or DKV App), or by (electronic) mail.  
          Takes all the necessary steps to obtain an intervention from the legal/statutory insurance as well as from the mutual
           fund (“mutuelle”).                                                     
              When and how do I pay?                                              
          The insurer sends you an invitation to pay your annual premium. You can request half-yearly, quarterly or monthly
          payments (via SEPA direct debit only) of your premium, in which case a splitting fee will be charged.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When does the cover start and end?                                  
                                                                                  
          Lifelong contract which cannot be terminated by the insurer except in the cases prescribed by law.
          The insurance guarantee starts upon policy issuance. The coverage starts upon expiration of applicable qualifying
          periods and first payment of the agreed premium. The Insurer may terminate the insurance contract in the event of non-
          payment of the premium.                                                 
                                                                                  
               How can I terminate the contract?                                  
                                                                                  
          As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying the Insurer
          no later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered letter, bailiff's
          writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.
                                                                                  
          This complimentary translation of an Insurance Product Information Document is provided for the sole purpose of informing
          (potential) customers in English. Only the French or Dutch language versions shall be authoritative between the contracting
          parties.

             Where am I covered)?                                                 
                                                                                  
          World-wide in case of unplanned / emergency hospitalisation.           
          European Union (except overseas countries and territories) in case of planned hospitalisation with prior approval of
           the legal/statutory health insurance (“mutuelle”).                     
                                                                                  
              What are my obligations?                                            
          Complete an administrative and medical questionnaire, except in the case of:
            affiliation of a newborn child within 60 days of its birth in an in-force contract, provided that one of its parents is
             insured under a DKV Hospi Premium which started prior to 01/01/19;   
            transfer to a DKV Hospi Premium insurance after having been insured for at least two continuous years in a
             Horizon plan.                                                        
           Notify the insurer in the event of a change of residence, social security status or a stay abroad of more than
            90 consecutive days.                                                  
          The reimbursement of certain benefits is subject to prior approval by the insurer :
            paramedical treatments other than nursing care, kinesitherapy, physiotherapy;
            medical devices,                                                     
            treatment plans for dental and prosthetic care,                      
            reconstructive plastic surgery.                                      
           Not subject to any prior approval: spectacle lenses, contact lenses, hearing aids, trusses for hernia, stockings for
           varicose veins, orthopaedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corset, splints and crutches.
          Medical adjuvants, medicines, bandages and medical equipment must be prescribed by a medical doctor.
          Each insurance claim must be reported to the insurer in writing within the designated period, preferably using the
           digital reporting tools (My DKV or DKV App), or by (electronic) mail.  
          Takes all the necessary steps to obtain an intervention from the legal/statutory insurance as well as from the mutual
           fund (“mutuelle”).                                                     
              When and how do I pay?                                              
          The insurer sends you an invitation to pay your annual premium. You can request half-yearly, quarterly or monthly
          payments (via SEPA direct debit only) of your premium, in which case a splitting fee will be charged.
                                                                                  
              When does the cover start and end?                                  
                                                                                  
          Lifelong contract which cannot be terminated by the insurer except in the cases prescribed by law.
          The insurance guarantee starts upon policy issuance. The coverage starts upon expiration of applicable qualifying
          periods and first payment of the agreed premium. The Insurer may terminate the insurance contract in the event of non-
          payment of the premium.                                                 
                                                                                  
               How can I terminate the contract?                                  
                                                                                  
          As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying the Insurer
          no later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered letter, bailiff's
          writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.
                                                                                  
          This complimentary translation of an Insurance Product Information Document is provided for the sole purpose of informing
          (potential) customers in English. Only the French or Dutch language versions shall be authoritative between the contracting
          parties.

              WHERE AM I COVERED?                                                 
                                                                                  
          "Medical costs" cover                                                  
             Cover in Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas territories): for any medically necessary, unforeseen
              hospitalization abroad                                              
             Cover in Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas territories): for any medically necessary and planned
              hospitalization abroad, provided a prior agreement has been obtained with the health insurance fund
             Cover in Belgium: for outpatient consultations and/or treatments in the pre- and post-hospitalization period of a covered
              hospitalization, within the context of "Critical Illnesses", of non-medical care support within the context of "Critical Illnesses"
          “Assistance abroad” DKV ASSISTANCE cover                               
           Cover in Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas territories): for any medically necessary, unforeseen
           hospitalization abroad provided that the insured person does not temporarily stay abroad for more than 90 consecutive days. This
           period is extended to 6 months in the case of temporary actual residence in Member States of the European Union (excluding
           overseas territories) of students, under the age of 26, when this is within the continuity of their studies under unilateral or multilateral
           programmes or an agreement between two or more institutions of higher education
              WHAT ARE MY OBLIGATIONS ?                                           
          Correctly complete and sign an insurance proposal, which consists of an administrative and medical questionnaire, except in case
           of individual continuation of a similar collective DKV hospitalization insurance
          Inform DKV within 30 days from when the conditions to maintain the insurance contract are no longer fulfilled. In particular:
           (1) any change of domicile or actual principal residence, (2) any stay abroad of more than 90 consecutive days or any temporary
           stay of more than 6 months in Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas territories) by insured students, under
           the age of 26, when they are undertaken in continuity of their studies under unilateral or multilateral programmes or an agreement
           between two or more institutions of higher education, (3) any change of social security status whereby the insured person is no
           longer subject to Belgian social security and can no longer benefit from it
          Take all reasonable measures to prevent and limit the consequences of an insurance case
          Undertake all necessary steps to obtain any intervention for all possible legal/statutory compensations, before requesting
           compensation from DKV                                                  
          Report any insurance case to DKV in accordance with the guidelines, terms and modalities described in the General Insurance
           Conditions: (1) by telephone on +32 2 230 31 32 for the “Assistance abroad” DKV ASSISTANCE cover, within 48 hours of the
           start of a hospitalization or due to a winter sports accident on a ski slope without hospitalization, or as soon as reasonably possible,
           (2) in writing for the "Medical costs" cover and preferably by using the digital customer portal (My DKV) or the smartphone
           application (DKV App) provided by DKV                                  
          Notify DKV of the existence of any other agreement that may give rise to a total or partial reimbursement of the actual borne costs
          Medical and paramedical treatments, medical devices, medicines, bandages and medical material on medical prescription by a
           physician                                                              
          Reimbursement of the costs listed below is subject to prior approval by DKV:
             Rental or purchase of medical devices, except spectacle glasses or contact lenses, fully non-implantable hearing aids,
              trusses for hernia, stockings for varicose veins, orthopedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corsets, splints and crutches;
             Treatment plan with accurate cost estimate for dental treatments;   
             Accurate cost estimate for reconstructive plastic surgery.          
              WHEN AND HOW DO I PAY?                                              
         DKV sends you an invitation to pay your annual premium. You can request half-yearly, quarterly or monthly payments (via SEPA
         direct debit only) of your premium, in which case a splitting fee will be charged.
              WHEN DOES THE COVER START AND END?                                  
         Term of the contract: lifetime cover, which cannot be cancelled by DKV except for the cases provided for by law.
         The insurance guarantee begins after issuance of the policy, after the expiration of the qualifying periods and after payment of the
         first agreed premium. DKV may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
              HOW CAN I CANCEL MY CONTRACT?                                       
         As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying DKV no later than three
         months before such date. The notification may only be realized by means of registered letter, bailiff's writ or handover of a
         cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.                     
        This complimentary translation of an Insurance Product Information Document is provided for the sole purpose of informing
        (potential) customers in English. Only the French or Dutch language versions shall be authoritative between the contracting
        parties.

              and planned hospitalization abroad, provided a prior agreement has been obtained with the health insurance
              fund                                                                
             Cover in Belgium: for outpatient consultations and/or treatments in the pre- and post-hospitalization period of a
              covered hospitalization, within the context of "Critical Illnesses", of non-medical care support within the context
              of "Critical Illnesses"                                             
           “Assistance abroad” DKV ASSISTANCE cover                              
             Cover in Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas territories): for any medically necessary,
              unforeseen hospitalization abroad provided that the insured person does not temporarily stay abroad for more
              than 90 consecutive days. This period is extended to 6 months in the case of temporary actual residence in
              Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas territories) of students, under the age of 26, when
              this is within the continuity of their studies under unilateral or multilateral programmes or an agreement between
              two or more institutions of higher education                        
             What are my obligations?                                             
          Correctly complete and sign an insurance proposal, which consists of an administrative and medical questionnaire
          Inform DKV within 30 days from when the conditions to maintain the insurance contract are no longer fulfilled. In
           particular: (1) any change of domicile or actual principal residence, (2) any stay abroad of more than 90 consecutive
           days or any temporary stay of more than 6 months in Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas
           territories) by insured students, under the age of 26, when they are undertaken in continuity of their studies under
           unilateral or multilateral programmes or an agreement between two or more institutions of higher education, (3) any
           change of social security status whereby the insured person is no longer subject to Belgian social security and can
           no longer benefit from it                                              
          Take all reasonable measures to prevent and limit the consequences of an insurance case
          Undertake all necessary steps to obtain any intervention for all possible legal/statutory compensations, before
           requesting compensation from DKV                                       
          Report any insurance case to DKV in accordance with the guidelines, terms and modalities described in the General
           Insurance Conditions: (1) by telephone on +32 2 230 31 32 for the “Assistance abroad” DKV ASSISTANCE cover,
           within 48 hours of the start of a hospitalization or due to a winter sports accident on a ski slope without hospitalization,
           or as soon as reasonably possible, (2) in writing for the "Medical costs" cover and preferably by using the digital
           customer portal (My DKV) or the smartphone application (DKV App) provided by DKV

           borne costs                                                            
          Medical and paramedical treatments, medical devices, medicines, bandages and medical material on medical
           prescription by a physician                                            
          Reimbursement of the costs listed below is subject to prior approval by DKV:
            Rental or purchase of medical devices, except spectacle glasses or contact lenses, fully non-implantable hearing
             aids, trusses for hernia, stockings for varicose veins, orthopedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corsets, splints and
             crutches;                                                            
            Treatment plan with accurate cost estimate for dental treatments;    
            Accurate cost estimate for reconstructive plastic surgery.           
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When and how do I pay?                                              
                                                                                  
         DKV sends you an invitation to pay your annual premium. You can request half-yearly, quarterly or monthly payments
         (via SEPA direct debit only) of your premium, in which case a splitting fee will be charged.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When does the cover start and end?                                  
                                                                                  
         Term of the contract: lifetime cover, which cannot be cancelled by DKV except for the cases provided for by law.
         The insurance guarantee begins after issuance of the policy, after the expiration of the qualifying periods and after
         payment of the first agreed premium. DKV may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              How can I cancel my contract?                                       
                                                                                  
         As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying DKV no
         later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realized by means of registered letter, bailiff's
         writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.

         parties.

         DKV sends you an invitation to pay your annual premium. You can request half-yearly, quarterly or monthly payments
         (via SEPA direct debit only) of your premium, in which case a splitting fee will be charged.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When does the cover start and end?                                  
                                                                                  
         Term of the contract: lifetime cover, which cannot be cancelled by DKV except for the cases provided for by law.
         The insurance guarantee begins after issuance of the policy, after the expiration of the qualifying periods and after
         payment of the first agreed premium. DKV may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              How can I cancel my contract?                                       
                                                                                  
         As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying DKV no

            Where am I covered?                                                   
                                                                                  
          "Medical costs" cover                                                  
             Worldwide cover: for any medically necessary, unforeseen hospitalization abroad. For hospitalizations outside
              the European Union, DKV reimburses up to a maximum of € 300.000 per insurance case.
             Cover in Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas territories): for any medically necessary
              and planned hospitalization abroad, provided a prior agreement has been obtained with the health insurance
              fund                                                                
             Cover in Belgium: for outpatient consultations and/or treatments in the pre- and post-hospitalization period of a
              covered hospitalization, within the context of "Critical Illnesses", of non-medical care support within the context
              of "Critical Illnesses", medically assisted procreation             
          “Assistance abroad” DKV ASSISTANCE cover                               
             Worldwide cover: for any medically necessary, unforeseen hospitalization abroad provided that the insured
              person does not temporarily stay abroad for more than 90 consecutive days. This period is extended to
              6 months in the case of temporary actual residence in Member States of the European Union (excluding
              overseas territories) of students, under the age of 26, when this is within the continuity of their studies under
              unilateral or multilateral programmes or an agreement between two or more institutions of higher education
             What are my obligations?                                             
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
          Correctly complete and sign an insurance proposal, which consists of an administrative and medical questionnaire,
           except in case of individual continuation of a similar collective DKV hospitalization insurance
          Inform DKV within 30 days from when the conditions to maintain the insurance contract are no longer fulfilled. In
           particular: (1) any change of domicile or actual principal residence, (2) any stay abroad of more than 90 consecutive
           days or any temporary stay of more than 6 months in Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas
           territories) by insured students, under the age of 26, when they are undertaken in continuity of their studies under
           unilateral or multilateral programmes or an agreement between two or more institutions of higher education, (3) any
           change of social security status whereby the insured person is no longer subject to Belgian social security and can
           no longer benefit from it                                              
          Take all reasonable measures to prevent and limit the consequences of an insurance case
          Undertake all necessary steps to obtain any intervention for all possible legal/statutory compensations, before
           requesting compensation from DKV

           Insurance Conditions: (1) by telephone on +32 2 230 31 32 for the “Assistance abroad” DKV ASSISTANCE cover,

           or as soon as reasonably possible, (2) in writing for the "Medical costs" cover and preferably by using the digital
           customer portal (My DKV) or the smartphone application (DKV App) provided by DKV

           medical prescription by a physician

              hearing aids, trusses for hernia, stockings for varicose veins, orthopedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corsets,
              splints and crutches;                                               
             Treatment plan with accurate cost estimate for dental treatments;   
             Accurate cost estimate for reconstructive plastic surgery.          
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When and how do I pay?                                              
                                                                                  
         DKV sends you an invitation to pay your annual premium. You can request half-yearly, quarterly or monthly payments
         (via SEPA direct debit only) of your premium, in which case a splitting fee will be charged.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When does the cover start and end?                                  
                                                                                  
         Term of the contract: lifetime cover, which cannot be cancelled by DKV except for the cases provided for by law.
         The insurance guarantee begins after issuance of the policy, after the expiration of the qualifying periods and after the
         payment of the first agreed premium. DKV may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              How can I cancel my contract?                                       
                                                                                  
         As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying DKV no
         later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realized by means of registered letter, bailiff's
         writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.

             Where am I covered) ?                                                
                                                                                  
          The insurance guarantee is valid in Belgium. In the specific case of emergency outpatient health care abroad, the
           insurance plan is also valid worldwide.                                
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              What are my obligations ?                                           
                                                                                  
          Correctly complete and sign an insurance proposal, which consists of an administrative and medical questionnaire
           (except in case of individual continuation of a similar corporate DKV outpatient health care insurance).
          Declare each insurance case to DKV in writing, preferably by means of the online customer portal or the
           smartphone application provided by DKV, using the declaration form(s) provided for this purpose and in accordance
           with the guidelines and modalities described.                          
          Request approval from DKV prior to the rent or purchase of any medical device, except for prescribed spectacle
           glasses or contact lenses, fully non-implantable hearing aids, trusses for hernia, stockings for varicose veins,
           orthopaedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corsets, splints and crutches.
          Prevent and limit the consequences of an insurance case.               
          Notify DKV of the existence of any other agreement that may give rise to a total or partial reimbursement of the
           actual borne costs.                                                    
          Undertake all necessary steps to obtain any intervention for all possible legal/statutory compensations, prior to
           claiming any request for compensation from DKV.                        
          Notify DKV within 30 days from the moment the conditions to maintain the insurance contract are no longer fulfilled.
           In particular: any change in actual residence or factual principal residence - any stay abroad of more than 90
           consecutive days or any temporary stay of more than 6 months in Member States of the European Union (excluding
           overseas territories) by students under the age of 26, when they are undertaken in continuity of their studies and
           enter into unilateral or multilateral programmes or within the framework of an agreement between two or more
           higher education institutions - any change of the social security status whereby the insured person is no longer
           subject to Belgian social security and can no longer benefit from it.  
              When and how do I pay ?                                             
          The insurer sends you an invitation to pay your annual premium. You can request half-yearly, quarterly or monthly
          payments (via SEPA direct debit only) of your premium, in which case a splitting fee will be charged.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When does the cover start and end ?                                 
                                                                                  
          Term of the contract: lifetime cover, which cannot be cancelled by the insurer except for the cases provided for by law.
          The insurance guarantee begins after issuing of the policy, after the expiration of the qualifying periods and after payment
          of the agreed premium. The insurer may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
               How can I terminate the contract ?                                 
          As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying the insurer
          no later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered letter, bailiff's
          writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.
                                                                                  
          This complimentary translation of an Insurance Product Information Document is provided for the sole purpose of informing
          (potential) customers in English. Only the French or Dutch language versions shall be authoritative between the contracting
          parties.

         Insurance Product Information Document                                   
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
         DKV Belgium S.A. / N.V.                                 PLAN GI          
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
         This information document is solely intended to provide you a summary of the key coverages and exclusions related to
         this product. This document is not adapted to your specific needs and the information within is not exhaustive. For more
         information about this product, your rights and obligations, consult the General, Tariff and/or Particular Insurance
         Conditions of this insurance. These documents are available at your insurance intermediary or free of charge from
         DKV Belgium S.A./N.V.                                 35 102_EN_5_202401 
                                                                                  
         What is this type of insurance?                                          
         Individual guaranteed income insurance that grants a revenue, in case of working disability in addition to the intervention
         of the Belgian social security, up to an insured annual annuity to compensate the loss or decrease in professional
         income of the insured due to an illness or an accident. This insurance can be concluded by any natural person or legal
         entity with domicile/registered office and permanent and usual residence in Belgium. This insurance is for anyone,
         between the ages of 18 and 55, who practices an insurable remunerated professional activity and has his domicile and
         his permanent and usual residence in Belgium.                            
                                                                                  
              What is insured?                 What is not insured?

          During the duration of working disability and after the                
           expiration of the deferred period, the coverage  Some dangerous non-professional or recreational
           consists of:                      sports activities (e.g. martial arts, defense, aerial
                                             sport (e.g. aviation), motor sports, etc)
           Monthly working disability annuity:  Intoxication, chronic or non-accidental toxicomania
            As from a working disability of at least 25%                         
            In proportion to the degree of working disability.                   
             A disability of 67% is considered as 100% (total                     
             working disability)                                                  
            Per expired month                                                    
           Premium reimbursement                                                  
            In case a monthly working disability annuity is                      
             paid

                                           ! Deferred period: period, during which the insurer is
                                             not bound to intervene, starting from the beginning
           Additional financial intervention ‘rehabilitation’                     
                                             of the working disability and of which the duration is
            From the 7th month of working disability provided                    
                                             chosen by the insured.               
             minimum 50% of working disability                                    
            For medical, paramedical or non-medical                              
             rehabilitation costs                                                 
            Up to the amount provided and being in effect for                    
             the insurance year (€ 1 500/year)                                    
          The Plan Business option ensures fixed business                        
           expenses up to a maximum of € 30,000/year to                           
           guarantee the continuity of professional activities for                
           the duration of the incapacity to work (limited to                     
           5 years per insurance case).                                           
       DKV Belgium S.A./N.V. | rue de Loxum 25 Loksumstraat | 1000 Bruxelles/Brussel | Tel.: +32 (0)2 287 64 11 | www.dkv.be
       R.P.M./ R.P.R. 0414858607 | Entreprise d’assurances agréée sous le n° 739, sous le contrôle de la Banque nationale de Belgique, dont le siège est situé à 1000 Bruxelles,
       Bld de Berlaimont 14 | Verzekeringsonderneming toegelaten onder het nr. 739, onder toezicht van de Nationale Bank van België, met zetel te 1000 Brussel, Berlaimontlaan 14

                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              Where am I covered?                                                 
                                                                                  
          Worldwide cover                                                        
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              What are my obligations?                                            
                                                                                  
          Filling out an administrative and medical questionnaire.               
          Undergo an additional medical examination if the annual annuity exceeds € 25 000.
          Provide proof of the professional income of the past 3 years (where applicable in function of the annual annuity).
          Inform the insurer in case of change of domicile/registered office or permanent and usual residence outside
          Belgium, of social security system and/or social security status, of practiced professional activities, the
          disappearance or resumption of professional activities, the existence of an agreement covering a similar or identical
          risk of any circumstance guaranteeing the maintenance of the insured’s professional income through his employer
          or otherwise, or benefiting from other financial interventions in case of working disability.
          Every insurance case (the occurrence, extension, change and the end) must be reported to the insurer
          electronically or in writing, using the designated declaration form and the within set out time limit.
                                                                                  
               When and how do I pay?                                             
                                                                                  
          The insurer sends you an invitation to pay your annual premium. You can request half-yearly, quarterly or monthly
          payments (via SEPA direct debit only) of your premium, in which case a splitting fee will be charged.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
               When does the cover start and end?                                 
                                                                                  
          Term of the contract: non-cancellable by the insurer except for the cases provided for by law. Automatically ends at the
          age of 67 at the latest, either at the end of the agreed reduced term, or at the definitive cessation of the insured’s
          professional activities or at the definitive disappearance of the insured’s professional income.
          The insurance guarantee begins after issuing of the policy, after the expiration of the deferred period and after payment
          of the agreed premium.                                                  
          The insurer may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              How can I cancel the contract?                                      
                                                                                  
          As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying the insurer
          no later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered letter, bailiff's
          writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
        This complimentary translation of an Insurance Product Information Document is provided for the sole purpose of
        informing (potential) customers in English. Only the French or Dutch language versions shall be authoritative between
        the contracting parties.

         WHERE AM I COVERED ?                                                     
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
        Worldwide cover: for an unplanned hospitalization.                       
        Cover in the Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas countries and territories). For a planned
         hospitalization abroad if a prior agreement has been obtained from the legal health insurance.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
          WHAT ARE MY OBLIGATIONS ?                                               
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
        No medical formalities.                                                  
        Enterprise with minimum 20 employees: the affiliation of all employees is compulsory. Family members can be affiliated under
         the same conditions at the expense or not of the employer.               
        Inform the insurer in case of change of domicile, change in social security status or of a stay abroad for more than
         90 consecutive days.                                                     
        Prior consent of the insurer for:                                        
         - paramedical treatments other than nursing care, kinesitherapy, physiotherapy;
         - medical devices.                                                       
         Not subject to any prior authorization: spectacle lenses, contact lenses, hearing aids, trusses for hernia, stockings for varicose
         veins, orthopedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corset, splints and crutches.
        Medical devices, medication, bandages and medical material on prescription from a physician.
        Each insurance claim must be reported to the insurer in writing within the designated period, preferably using the digital
         reporting tools (My DKV or DKV App), or by (electronic) mail.            
        The insured undertakes all steps to obtain reimbursement from his/her health insurance fund.
                                                                                  
         WHEN AND HOW DO I PAY ?                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
       DKV sends you an invitation to pay your premium, which is payable at the agreed periodicity.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
         WHEN DOES THE COVER START AND END ?                                      
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
       Term of the contract: yearly renewable contract according to the conditions in force.
       The insurance guarantee begins after issuing of the policy and after payment of the agreed premium.
       The insurer may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
         HOW  CAN I CANCEL THE CONTRACT ?                                         
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
       As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying the Insurer no later
       than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered letter, bailiff's writ or handover
       of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
       This complimentary translation of an Insurance Product Information Document is provided for the sole purpose of informing
       (potential) customers in English. Only the French or Dutch language versions shall be authoritative between the contracting
       parties.

           digital reporting tools (My DKV or DKV App), or by (electronic) mail.  
          The insured undertakes all steps to obtain reimbursement from his/her health insurance fund.
              When and how do I pay?                                              
                                                                                  
          The insurer sends the employer an invitation to pay the premium, which is payable at the agreed periodicity.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When does the cover start and end?                                  
                                                                                  
          Term of the contract: yearly renewable contract according to the conditions in force.
          The insurance guarantee begins after issuing of the policy and after payment of the agreed premium.
          The insurer may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
                                                                                  
              How can I cancel my contract?                                       
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
          As prescribed by law, the employer may terminate the insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying the
          insurer no later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered letter,
          bailiff's writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.

           expense or not of the employer.

           90 consecutive days.

            medical devices.

           provided that the dioptre has changed with at least 0.5), hearing aids, trusses for hernia, stockings for varicose veins,

           digital reporting tools (My DKV or DKV App), or by (electronic) mail.  
          The insured undertakes all steps to obtain reimbursement from his/her health insurance fund.
              When and how do I pay?                                              
          The insurer sends the employer an invitation to pay the premium, which is payable at the agreed periodicity.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              When does the cover start and end?                                  
                                                                                  
          Term of the contract: yearly renewable contract according to the conditions in force.
          The insurance guarantee begins after issuing of the policy and, elapsing of the qualifying periods and after payment of
          the first agreed premium. The insurer may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
                                                                                  
              How can I cancel my contract?                                       
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
          As prescribed by law, the employer may terminate the insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying the
          insurer no later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered letter,
          bailiff's writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.

         the affiliation of all employees is compulsory unless the contract states otherwise. Family members can be affiliated
          under the same conditions at the expense or not of the employer.        
         Inform the insurer in case of change of domicile, change in social security status or of a stay abroad for more than
          90 consecutive days.                                                    
         Prior consent of the insurer for:                                       
           paramedical treatments other than nursing care, kinesitherapy, physiotherapy;
           medical devices;                                                      
           orthodontic and/or prosthetic treatment.                              
         Not subject to any prior authorization: spectacle glasses, contact lenses, hearing aids, trusses for hernia, stockings
          for varicose veins, orthopedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corset, splints and crutches
         Medical devices, medication, bandages and medical material on prescription from a physician.
         Each insurance claim must be reported to the insurer in writing within the designated period, preferably using the
          digital reporting tools (My DKV or DKV App), or by (electronic) mail.   
         The insured undertakes all steps to obtain reimbursement from his/her health insurance fund.
                                                                                  
            When and how do I pay?                                                
                                                                                  
         the insurer sends the employer an invitation to pay the premium, which is payable at the agreed periodicity.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
             When does the cover start and end?                                   
                                                                                  
         Term of the contract: yearly renewable contract according to the conditions in force.
         The insurance guarantee begins after issuing of the policy and after the expiration of the qualifying periods and after
         payment of the agreed premium. The insurer may cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.
         The coverage starts for the insureds at the moment when the affiliation is in effect.
                                                                                  
              How can I cancel my contract?                                       
                                                                                  
          As prescribed by law, the employer may terminate the insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying
          the Insurer no later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered
          letter, bailiff's writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
        This complimentary translation of an Insurance Product Information Document is provided for the sole purpose of
        informing (potential) customers in English. Only the French or Dutch language versions shall be authoritative between
        the contracting parties.

       DKV Belgium S.A./N.V. | rue de Loxum 25 Loksumstraat | 1000 Bruxelles/Brussel | Tel.: +32 (0)2 287 64 11 | www.dkv.be

       Bld de Berlaimont 14 | Verzekeringsonderneming toegelaten onder het nr. 739, onder toezicht van de Nationale Bank van België, met zetel te 1000 Brussel, Berlaimontlaan 14

                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
              Are there any restrictions on cover?                                
         ! Reimbursement at 50% of covered expenses on the entire hospital bill if the legal health insurance does not intervene
           on none of the items of the hospital bill.                             
         ! Hospitalization with overnight stay in a single room: a personal contribution of € 150 will remain at the insured’s
           charge for each hospitalization. A relapse that results in a new admission is considered a new hospitalization.
         ! Day-care is reimbursed in a single room at the rate of an admission to a double room.
         ! When there is no intervention of the legal health insurance for medicines, medical devices and prostheses, artificial
           limbs, bandages and medical material on the hospital bill: reimbursement at 100% up to an amount of € 10 000 per
           insured and per insurance year and everything above this amount at 50%.
         ! Reimbursement at 50% when there is no intervention of the legal health insurance for pre-post and Critical Illnesses
         ! Expenses of a wig with a maximum of € 1 250                            
         ! In case of psychotherapeutic admission and treatment in a hospital, reimbursement is limited to 120 days per insured
           and per insurance year                                                 
         ! Reimbursement of psychotherapeutic treatment if provided by a psychiatrist
         ! Maximum intervention outpatient and dental costs without hospitalization: the described expenses are cumulatively
           reimbursed up to the insured annual capital of € 2.500 per insured person and per insurance year
         ! Waiting period of 36 months between 2 spectacle frames                 
              Where am I covered ?                                                
           Worldwide cover: for an unplanned hospitalization and unplanned outpatient and dental treatments.
           Cover in the Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas countries and territories): For a planned
            hospitalization abroad if a prior agreement has been obtained from the legal health insurance.
           Cover in the Member States of the European Union (excluding overseas countries and territories): for planned
            outpatient and dental treatments if prior agreement has been obtained from the legal health insurance and the
            insurer.                                                              
             What are my obligations ?                                            
          No medical formalities.                                                
          The affiliation of all independent managers is compulsory. Family members can be affiliated under the same
           conditions.                                                            
          Inform the insurer in case of change of domicile, change in social security status or of a stay abroad for more than
           90 consecutive days.                                                   
          Prior consent of the insurer for:                                      
              paramedical treatments other than nursing care, kinesitherapy, physiotherapy;
              medical devices.                                                   
              orthodontic and/or prosthetic treatment.                           
          Not subject to any prior authorization: spectacle lenses, contact lenses, hearing aids, trusses for hernia, stockings for
           varicose veins, orthopedic soles, plaster casts, lumbar corset, splints and crutches.
          Prescription from a physician for medical devices, medication, bandages and medical material.
          Each insurance claim must be reported to the insurer in writing within the designated period, preferably using the
           digital reporting tools (My DKV or DKV App), or by (electronic) mail.  
          The insured undertakes all steps to obtain reimbursement from his/her health insurance fund.
             When and how do I pay?                                               
          DKV sends you an invitation to pay your premium, which is payable at the agreed periodicity.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
             When does the cover start and end?                                   
         Term of the contract: yearly renewable contract according to the conditions in force. The contract ends no later than
         when the independent manager reaches the age of 67. The insurance guarantee begins after issuing of the policy, after
         expiration of the qualifying periods and after payment of the first payment of the agreed premium. The insurer may
         cancel the insurance contract in case of non-payment of the premium.     
                                                                                  
              How can I cancel my contract?                                       
                                                                                  
          As prescribed by law, you may terminate your insurance contract at every annual renewal date by notifying the Insurer
          no later than three months before such date. The notification may only be realised by means of registered letter,
          bailiff's writ or handover of a cancellation letter with acknowledgement of receipt.
                                                                                  
                                                                                  
      This complimentary translation of an Insurance Product Information Document is provided for the sole purpose of
      informing (potential) customers in English. Only the French or Dutch language versions shall be authoritative between
      the contracting parties.
